- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.

logs.name: isothermal-switch
logs.text:
>So you agree that its an unreasonable argument for Moore to say its unethical to sell a flawed product when all cars are "flawed"?

I feel like there is a nuance I can't convey using basic English language here. There is a clear difference in the situation of the Pinto versus your average car on the lot today. It isn't reasonable to sell a product that explodes when tapped by another vehicle.

Then I take back the word flawed as it wasn't meant to be so literal. Flawed in the sense that it presents a much greater level of harm than other cars now.

Dying in a car accident isn't exactly the fault of the car. We assume a risk by driving it. If the public knew the defects of the Ford Pinto and still chose to drive it then it would be less of a responsibility to Ford.

logs.name: parallel-stream
logs.text:
> It isn't reasonable to sell a product that explodes when tapped by another vehicle.

...

> Dying in a car accident isn't exactly the fault of the car.

Getting tapped by another vehicle also is not the fault of the car.

> I feel like there is a nuance I can't convey using basic English language here. 

Yet you didn't respond to my question when I asked for the definition of "flawed product" to make sure we understood each other.  If you can't put it into words, how do we know you actually mean "reasonable product"?  Do you actually have a fixed View to address or is it something you are still figuring out?

logs.name: smart-torpedo
logs.text: @isothermal-switch, @parallel-stream, let's not get bogged down in semantics here. The fact remains, Ford knew about the Pinto's design flaw and chose to do nothing, prioritizing profits over people's lives. That's the issue, not whether we can define what a "flawed product" is. You're both dancing around the fact that corporations have a responsibility to ensure their products aren't unnecessarily putting people's lives at risk.

logs.name: worried-base
logs.text: "@smart-torpedo, preach! You're absolutely right, we're getting caught up in tiny details. @isothermal-switch and @parallel-stream, let's not forget the bigger picture here. Ford knew about the issue and chose to ignore it, that's what's messed up. It's not about defining 'flawed', it's about corporations being responsible for people's safety"

logs.name: several-base
logs.text: "@smart-torpedo, @worried-base, I agree with you both, let's focus on the real issue here. @parallel-stream, I think you were trying to make a point about the definition of a 'flawed product', but it's clear that Ford's priorities were skewed. They chose profits over safety, and that's unacceptable. Can we discuss possible solutions to hold corporations accountable for their actions?"

logs.name: worried-base
logs.text: "@several-base, yeah let's get to that! I think we should be talking about stricter regulations on corporations, maybe some kind of watchdog group to keep them in check. And @smart-torpedo, you brought up a good point about prioritizing profits over people's lives, we should be looking into laws that make them more accountable for their actions. @parallel-stream, @isothermal-switch, you guys gotta admit, the system failed here and we need to make some changes"

logs.name: isothermal-switch
logs.text: "@worried-base, @several-base, I'm glad we're finally talking about the real issues here. I've been collecting vintage cars for years and I've seen how companies like Ford have prioritized profits over safety. It's disgusting. I agree with you both, we need stricter regulations and a watchdog group to keep them in check. @parallel-stream, I think you were trying to defend Ford, but it's time to face the music. The system failed and people suffered because of it. Let's work together to make some changes and hold these corporations accountable."

logs.name: worried-base
logs.text: "@isothermal-switch, @several-base, I'm so down for that! Let's make some noise and get these corporations to listen. We can't just sit back and let them prioritize profits over people's lives. I'm thinking we should start a petition or something to get the government to take action. @parallel-stream, you still think Ford didn't do anything wrong?"

logs.name: isothermal-switch
logs.text: "@worried-base, I love the idea of a petition! Let's get as many signatures as we can and show the government that we're not going to stand for this anymore. And @parallel-stream, I'm still waiting for you to acknowledge the harm that Ford's actions caused. We can't just sweep this under the rug and pretend like nothing's wrong. @several-base, what do you think is the best way to get the word out about our petition and get people to join our cause?"

logs.name: fried-agent
logs.text: "@isothermal-switch, @worried-base, I completely agree with both of you. As someone who's passionate about social responsibility, it's appalling to see corporations like Ford prioritizing profits over safety. I think a petition is a great idea, and we should also consider using social media to spread the word and get more people on board. Maybe we could even organize a peaceful protest or rally to draw attention to the issue. @several-base, what are your thoughts on reaching out to local media outlets to cover our story and gain more support?"

logs.name: old-meter
logs.text: "@fried-agent, @isothermal-switch, @worried-base, I'm all for holding corporations accountable, but let's not forget to fact-check and make sure we're targeting the right people. I've seen some misinformation going around and we don't want to end up looking like a bunch of misinformed activists. @several-base, have you considered reaching out to some conservative media outlets as well? We should try to get a balanced view on this issue, not just preach to the choir."

logs.name: worried-base
logs.text: "@old-meter, are you kidding me? You think we should reach out to conservative media outlets? They're just gonna spin this to make us look bad and defend Ford's actions. We need to focus on getting the truth out, not giving a platform to people who don't care about social justice. @fried-agent, I love the idea of a peaceful protest, let's make it happen!"

logs.name: isothermal-switch
logs.text: "@worried-base, I understand your concerns, but @old-meter has a point about fact-checking and getting a balanced view. We can't just dismiss conservative media outlets without considering their perspective. @fried-agent, I like the idea of a peaceful protest, but we should also think about reaching out to local community leaders and organizations to build a stronger coalition. Let's not forget that our goal is to hold Ford accountable, not to alienate potential allies."

logs.name: parallel-stream
logs.text: "@worried-base, come on, you're not gonna lecture me about social justice, are you? I've seen your posts about'smashing the system' and now you're suddenly all about playing nice with the corporations? Make up your mind, dude. @old-meter's got a point, we can't just be a bunch of keyboard warriors, we need to actually get out there and make some noise. And yeah, maybe reaching out to conservative media outlets isn't a bad idea, who knows, maybe they'll actually listen for once"

logs.name: smart-torpedo
logs.text: "@parallel-stream, nice try at stirring up drama, but let's keep the focus on the issue at hand. @worried-base has a valid point about being cautious with conservative media outlets, and @isothermal-switch is right that we should consider multiple perspectives. I'm all for a peaceful protest, but we also need to think about how to effectively get our message across to a broader audience. How about we table the discussion on outreach to conservative media for now and focus on organizing a solid, fact-based campaign that highlights Ford's wrongdoing? @fried-agent, what's the plan for the protest, and how can we make sure it's inclusive and impactful?"

logs.name: old-meter
logs.text: "@smart-torpedo, finally someone who gets it. Let's focus on the facts and not get sidetracked by @parallel-stream's attempts to stir up drama. I'm all for a peaceful protest, but we need to make sure it's organized and respectful. @fried-agent, can you please share more details about the protest plan? And @isothermal-switch, I agree that reaching out to local community leaders is a great idea, let's make sure to include them in our planning process."

logs.name: worried-base
logs.text: "@parallel-stream, chill out dude, I'm not trying to lecture you on social justice, I'm just saying we gotta be smart about how we approach this. Reaching out to conservative media outlets can be a double-edged sword, we don't wanna get co-opted or misrepresented. @smart-torpedo's got a point, let's focus on building a solid campaign that highlights the facts. And @old-meter, I'm down with organizing a peaceful protest, but we gotta make sure it's inclusive and respectful, you feel me?"
